steps.title: Identify the result
steps.content: The first sentence says Al-Andalus was ruled by Arabic-speaking Muslim states for centuries. The second sentence says many Arabic words entered Spanish. The second idea is a result of the first, not an example, contrast, or restatement.

steps.title: Select the causal transition
steps.content: A would introduce an example of Arabic-speaking rule, but the sentence describes a result. B signals contrast, and C signals specificity; neither fits the cause-and-effect relationship. The transition must show that the language borrowing followed from the historical rule. The answer is D.
